channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Kitty cats and cloud hands - how U.S. Olympic snowboarders keep calm in competition
item.description: U.S. snowboarders psych themselves up before competition with heavy metal and pop music, cat photos, and apparently many on the men's halfpipe team no...

item.title: A familiar move with a new twist: Trump tries to cut CDC funds he just signed into law
item.description: A federal judge in Illinois quickly issued a restraining order after the Trump administration slashed more than $600 million in CDC grants to four blu...

item.title: FBI releases description of suspect, increases reward in Nancy Guthrie case
item.description: The FBI describes the armed man caught on Nancy Guthrie's camera as 5-foot-9-inches to 5-foot-10 and of average build. The 84-year-old mother of Savan...

item.title: Minnesota immigration crackdown will end, border czar says. And, DHS funding to expire
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan announced that the Trump administration will end the immigration crackdown in Minnesota. And, DHS funding is set to expire after...

item.title: Virginia court allows Democrats' redistricting vote in their plan to counter to Trump
item.description: The ruling allows an April election where voters can let the legislature draw a new congressional map. It could help Democrats win more House seats. R...

item.title: The SEC closed its investigation into Fisker
item.description: The financial regulator revealed in a response to a TechCrunch records request that the probe was closed in September 2025.

item.title: Sex toys maker Tenga says hacker stole customer information
item.description: The Japanese sex toy maker said a hacker broke into an employee's inbox and stole customer names, email addresses, and correspondence, including order...

item.title: How to Buy Rental #2, #3, or #4 When You’re Out of Funds (Rookie Reply)
item.description: Stuck at one rental property? Maybe you spent years saving for that first down payment, and now, your funds are depleted. Where do you go from here? N...

item.title: Journalist Don Lemon pleads not guilty to civil rights charges in Minnesota church protest
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon has pleaded not guilty to federal civil rights charges in Minnesota, accused in a protest at a church in St.

item.title: Fintech lending giant Figure confirms data breach
item.description: The company said hackers downloaded “a limited number of files” after breaking into an employee’s account. The hacking group ShinyHunters took respons...

item.title: Minnesotans welcome the immigration surge drawdown but remain vigilant
OpenAI removes access to sycophancy-prone GPT-4o model
item.description: The model is known for its overly sycophantic nature and its role in several lawsuits involving users' unhealthy relationships to the chatbot.

item.title: Why top talent is walking away from OpenAI and xAI
item.description: AI companies have been hemorrhaging talent the past few weeks. Half of xAI’s founding team has left the company — some on their own, others through “r...

item.title: How to (Legally) Pay the Least Amount in Taxes as a Real Estate Investor
item.description: This episode alone could save you hundreds, thousands, or tens of thousands in taxes—all with 100% legal means. If you own a rental property, you coul...

item.title: Feds open a perjury probe into ICE officers’ testimony about the shooting of a Venezuelan man
Grand Rapids Could Become a Boomtown as Investment Money Pours In
item.description: Looking for an affordable, cash-flowing market earmarked for major development and a projected population and economic surge? Grand Rapids, Michigan, ...

item.title: Buying (and Building) Houses Could Get a LOT Easier (New Bill)
item.description: This could be the most important shift in the housing market in years. Something truly remarkable just happened that will impact almost every facet of...

item.title: Over 60% of Buyers Bought Below Asking Price Last Year, With the Largest Discounts Since 2012
item.description: For most of us, the frenzied bidding wars and constant price hikes of the post-pandemic housing boom are recent memories. That’s why it might come as ...
